note: Adapted /improve for the loop task: list all stubs, implement them, research docs/vision, ask when unclear. Most 'stub' textual matches are legitimate test infrastructure (stub_embed, deterministic bench embedders, distill test fixtures) or UI placeholders — excluded. Below are GENUINE production stubs / incomplete paths.

stubs.verdict: KEEP + WIRE. Decisive: src/config/graph.rs:23 reserves huge-corpus scaling as 'the DiskANN index's job'; src/base/constants.rs:69 ships NO entity-eviction cap, so the graph-global in-RAM HnswIndex (entity_idx/gnn_entity_idx/reason_idx, populated by rebuild_index for all loaded kerns) grows UNBOUNDED. User confirmed db grows unbounded in practice. DiskANN fixes the resident-RAM/load-time ceiling (vectors mmap'd on disk, not heap). Not on-disk byte count (that'd be PQ/GC).
stubs.next_action: I1 DONE + I2 DONE (uncommitted). I2: GraphGnn::build_entity_disk_index(dir) in src/base/graph.rs snapshots resident entities (BTreeMap id-sorted/deduped, skip Superseded, f64->f32) via diskann::build_and_save; parity test disk_index_snapshot_mirrors_in_ram_membership_and_ranking GREEN; full base:: suite 146/146 pass. NEXT = I3: introduce VectorBackend enum (Resident(HnswIndex) | Disk{snapshot,delta,tombstones}) and route src/base/search.rs through it with NO behavior change (always Resident). Keep entire existing search suite green. Then I4 delta+tombstones, I5 rebuild_index threshold + [graph] disk_threshold config.
stubs.doc_reconciled: docs/kern/diskann-disk-index.md Status updated to reflect LMDB reality + KEEP/WIRE decision. TODO: correct kern memory 'retrieval-stack-state' (claimed wired) once kern MCP reconnects — it was DOWN this turn.
stubs.evidence: Module header: 'self-contained and tested, but not yet wired into the live search path'. Live path is src/base/search.rs (search_all_unlocked/_filtered over GraphGnn in-memory HnswIndex). diskann.rs = read-only mmap Vamana over full f32 vectors (NO PQ yet). Design doc docs/kern/diskann-disk-index.md is STALE: its Phase-1 target (replace cold.rs O(n) JSONL scan) is obsolete — cold.rs/persist.rs were replaced by LMDB store.rs (int8-on-disk); cold_search (store.rs:432) is now an explicitly BOUNDED scan (capped by COLD_MAX_ENTRIES), so the linear-scan motivation was deliberately neutralized. Phase-2 (disk-backed hot index for large kerns) is the real value but high-risk and wants PQ-in-RAM that current diskann.rs lacks.
stubs.note: PREMISE PARTLY STALE. The LMDB refactor (int8-on-disk) already attacked the RAM ceiling the doc worried about, and the cold cap removed Phase-1's reason to exist. Wiring this 490-line subsystem into the hot path on a stale premise is questionable. Re-asked user with grounded fork: (A) Phase-2 hot wiring (+PQ, large multi-session), (B) reconcile docs/memory + defer DiskANN as a ready building-block, move loop to next real stub, (C) smallest self-contained slice = optional DiskIndex accelerator for cold_search. NEEDS USER DECISION.

stubs.evidence: Traced end-to-end: tools_mutate.rs:224 async path returns worker.enqueue(...) = util::content_hash(text) (worker.rs:50). Worker commits the entity under the SAME content hash (worker.rs:122). Test enqueue_returns_the_content_hash_doc_id pins this invariant. The id is stable and resolvable on a later read — NOT a throwaway placeholder.
stubs.resolution: No logic stub. Only defect was the misleading word 'placeholder' in dto.rs doc comments (IngestReq.sync, IngestRes.entity_id). Reworded both to state it is the stable content-hash doc id the worker commits under. Doc-only change.

stubs.evidence: 'Closing does not remove the document; sessions stay searchable post-close. No-op for now.'
stubs.note: Reads as intentional design, not an unfinished stub. Likely no action.
